title_en: The Problem of Legal Stratagems and Their Refutation
content_en:
One of the longest and most polemical sections of I'lam al-Muwaqqi'in is Ibn al-Qayyim's comprehensive refutation of legal stratagems (hiyal): devices by which scholars constructed technically valid transactions or arrangements that circumvented the intent of the Shariah while satisfying its formal conditions. The practice of hiyal had a long history in Islamic jurisprudence, particularly in the Hanafi school, where a significant literature of hiyal texts had developed. Ibn al-Qayyim's attack on this literature is thorough, drawing on Quranic principles, hadith evidence, the practice of the Companions, and logical argumentation. He regards hiyal not as a legitimate tool of legal reasoning but as a corruption of the law that is more dangerous than open transgression, because it clothes sin in the garb of scholarship.

The riba example is central to Ibn al-Qayyim's critique. The prohibition of interest (riba) in the Quran and Sunnah is among the most explicit and serious prohibitions in Islamic law; the Quran declares that those who consume riba are at war with Allah and His Messenger. Yet certain jurists had developed contract structures that produced the economic effect of an interest-bearing loan while avoiding the technical definition of riba: for example, a 'double sale' in which a lender sells goods to a borrower at a high deferred price and then buys them back at a lower immediate price, effectively extracting a fixed return on the capital advanced. Ibn al-Qayyim argues that these structures are prohibited not in spite of their technical cleverness but because of it: they demonstrate a deliberate intention to achieve what Allah has prohibited, and intention is precisely what the Shariah evaluates.

Ibn al-Qayyim develops a general principle from this analysis: acts are judged by their intentions and purposes, both in the divine reckoning and in the assessment of legal scholars. This principle, drawn from the famous hadith of Umar ibn al-Khattab on intentions, applies not only to acts of worship but to transactions and social arrangements. A transaction whose purpose is to produce a prohibited outcome is a prohibited transaction, regardless of how its formal elements are structured. This means that the jurist cannot evaluate a contract merely by examining its formal conditions; he must ask what the parties intended to achieve and whether that intended outcome is something the Shariah permits. Ibn al-Qayyim traces this principle through the rulings of the Companions and the major jurists of the early generations, showing that it was well established before the hiyal literature constructed its elaborate circumventions.

The broader target of Ibn al-Qayyim's critique is a conception of legal scholarship that reduces fiqh to a technical game of formal categories, in which the scholar's task is to find arrangements that satisfy the required conditions rather than to serve the purposes for which those conditions were established. He contrasts this with what he regards as the authentic tradition: scholars who understood that their task was to help people live in accordance with the divine law's actual requirements, not to help them achieve prohibited ends by roundabout paths. The legacy of this section has been substantial. Ibn al-Qayyim's arguments against hiyal have been taken up by scholars of Islamic finance and law who seek to distinguish genuine Shariah compliance from formal compliance that masks conventional interest-bearing finance. The question of whether a transaction is substantively permissible or merely formally so remains one of the most contested in contemporary Islamic legal practice.
content_id:
Salah satu bagian terpanjang dan paling polemis dari I'lam Al-Muwaqqiin adalah penolakan komprehensif Ibnu Al-Qayyim terhadap hiyal: perangkat-perangkat yang dengannya para ulama membangun transaksi-transaksi atau pengaturan-pengaturan yang secara teknis valid yang mengelak dari maksud syariat sambil memenuhi syarat-syarat formalnya. Praktik hiyal memiliki sejarah panjang dalam fikih Islam, terutama dalam mazhab Hanafi, di mana literatur hiyal yang signifikan telah berkembang. Serangan Ibnu Al-Qayyim terhadap literatur ini menyeluruh, mengandalkan prinsip-prinsip Al-Quran, bukti hadis, praktik para Sahabat, dan argumentasi logis. Beliau memandang hiyal bukan sebagai alat penalaran hukum yang sah melainkan sebagai perusakan hukum yang lebih berbahaya dari pelanggaran terbuka, karena ia mendandani dosa dengan jubah keilmuan.

Contoh riba adalah inti dari kritik Ibnu Al-Qayyim. Larangan riba dalam Al-Quran dan Sunnah termasuk di antara larangan-larangan yang paling eksplisit dan serius dalam hukum Islam; Al-Quran menyatakan bahwa mereka yang memakan riba berada dalam keadaan perang dengan Allah dan Rasul-Nya. Namun, ahli fikih tertentu telah mengembangkan struktur-struktur kontrak yang menghasilkan efek ekonomi dari pinjaman berbunga sambil menghindari definisi teknis riba: misalnya, 'jual beli ganda' di mana pemberi pinjaman menjual barang kepada peminjam dengan harga tangguh yang tinggi lalu membelinya kembali dengan harga tunai yang lebih rendah, yang secara efektif mengekstrak imbal hasil tetap atas modal yang dipinjamkan. Ibnu Al-Qayyim berpendapat bahwa struktur-struktur ini dilarang bukan meskipun kecerdasan teknisnya tetapi justru karena itu: mereka menunjukkan niat yang disengaja untuk mencapai apa yang Allah telah larang, dan niat adalah justru apa yang dievaluasi syariat.

Ibnu Al-Qayyim mengembangkan prinsip umum dari analisis ini: perbuatan-perbuatan dihakimi berdasarkan niat-niat dan tujuan-tujuannya, baik dalam perhitungan ilahi maupun dalam penilaian para ulama hukum. Prinsip ini, yang diambil dari hadis terkenal Umar bin Al-Khaththab tentang niat-niat, berlaku tidak hanya untuk ibadah tetapi untuk transaksi-transaksi dan pengaturan-pengaturan sosial. Transaksi yang tujuannya adalah menghasilkan hasil yang dilarang adalah transaksi yang dilarang, terlepas dari bagaimana elemen-elemen formalnya disusun. Ini berarti bahwa ahli fikih tidak dapat mengevaluasi suatu kontrak semata-mata dengan memeriksa syarat-syarat formalnya; ia harus bertanya apa yang ingin dicapai oleh para pihak dan apakah hasil yang dimaksudkan itu sesuatu yang diizinkan syariat. Ibnu Al-Qayyim menelusuri prinsip ini melalui hukum-hukum para Sahabat dan ahli fikih utama generasi awal, menunjukkan bahwa prinsip itu sudah mapan sebelum literatur hiyal membangun pengelakan-pengelakannya yang rumit.

Target lebih luas dari kritik Ibnu Al-Qayyim adalah konsepsi keilmuan hukum yang mereduksi fikih menjadi permainan teknis kategori-kategori formal, di mana tugas ulama adalah menemukan pengaturan-pengaturan yang memenuhi syarat-syarat yang diperlukan daripada melayani tujuan-tujuan yang untuk itu syarat-syarat tersebut ditetapkan. Beliau membandingkan ini dengan apa yang beliau anggap sebagai tradisi yang otentik: para ulama yang memahami bahwa tugas mereka adalah membantu orang-orang hidup sesuai dengan persyaratan-persyaratan aktual hukum ilahi, bukan membantu mereka mencapai tujuan-tujuan yang dilarang melalui jalan-jalan berputar. Warisan bagian ini sangat besar. Argumen-argumen Ibnu Al-Qayyim melawan hiyal telah diambil oleh para ulama keuangan dan hukum Islam yang berusaha membedakan kepatuhan syariah yang sejati dari kepatuhan formal yang menyembunyikan keuangan konvensional berbunga. Pertanyaan apakah suatu transaksi secara substantif diizinkan atau sekadar secara formal tetap menjadi salah satu yang paling diperdebatkan dalam praktik hukum Islam kontemporer.
content_ar:
One of the longest and most polemical sections of I'lam al-Muwaqqi'in is Ibn al-Qayyim's comprehensive refutation of legal stratagems (hiyal): devices by which scholars constructed technically valid transactions or arrangements that circumvented the intent of the Shariah while satisfying its formal conditions. The practice of hiyal had a long history in Islamic jurisprudence, particularly in the Hanafi school, where a significant literature of hiyal texts had developed. Ibn al-Qayyim's attack on this literature is thorough, drawing on Quranic principles, الحديث evidence, the practice of the الصحابة, and logical argumentation. He regards hiyal not as a legitimate tool of legal reasoning but as a corruption of the law that is more dangerous than open transgression, because it clothes sin in the garb of scholarship.

The riba example is central to Ibn al-Qayyim's critique. The prohibition of interest (riba) in the القرآن الكريم and السنة النبوية is among the most explicit and serious prohibitions in Islamic law; the القرآن الكريم declares that those who consume riba are at war with الله and His Messenger. Yet certain jurists had developed contract structures that produced the economic effect of an interest-bearing loan while avoiding the technical definition of riba: for example, a 'double sale' in which a lender sells goods to a borrower at a high deferred price and then buys them back at a lower immediate price, effectively extracting a fixed return on the capital advanced. Ibn al-Qayyim argues that these structures are prohibited not in spite of their technical cleverness but because of it: they demonstrate a deliberate intention to achieve what الله has prohibited, and intention is precisely what the Shariah evaluates.

Ibn al-Qayyim develops a general principle from this analysis: acts are judged by their intentions and purposes, both in the divine reckoning and in the assessment of legal scholars. This principle, drawn from the famous الحديث of Umar ibn al-Khattab on intentions, applies not only to acts of worship but to transactions and social arrangements. A transaction whose purpose is to produce a prohibited outcome is a prohibited transaction, regardless of how its formal elements are structured. This means that the jurist cannot evaluate a contract merely by examining its formal conditions; he must ask what the parties intended to achieve and whether that intended outcome is something the Shariah permits. Ibn al-Qayyim traces this principle through the rulings of the الصحابة and the major jurists of the early generations, showing that it was well established before the hiyal literature constructed its elaborate circumventions.

The broader target of Ibn al-Qayyim's critique is a conception of legal scholarship that reduces الفقه to a technical game of formal categories, in which the scholar's task is to find arrangements that satisfy the required conditions rather than to serve the purposes for which those conditions were established. He contrasts this with what he regards as the authentic tradition: scholars who understood that their task was to help people live in accordance with the divine law's actual requirements, not to help them achieve prohibited ends by roundabout paths. The legacy of this section has been substantial. Ibn al-Qayyim's arguments against hiyal have been taken up by scholars of Islamic finance and law who seek to distinguish genuine Shariah compliance from formal compliance that masks conventional interest-bearing finance. The question of whether a transaction is substantively permissible or merely formally so remains one of the most contested in contemporary Islamic legal practice.
